content:
First write \(n=[r]\in\mathbb{Z}_{\ge0}\) and \(f=\{r\}\in[0,1)\).  Then the equation 
\[
25f+n=125
\]
gives 
\[
f=\frac{125-n}{25}\,.
\]
Since \(0\le f<1\) we require
\[
0\le\frac{125-n}{25}<1
\;\Longrightarrow\;
100<n\le125,
\]
so \(n=101,102,\dots,125\).  For each such \(n\),
\[
r=n+f
=n+\frac{125-n}{25}
=\frac{24n+125}{25}.
\]
Hence the sum of all solutions is
\[
\sum_{n=101}^{125}r
=\sum_{n=101}^{125}\frac{24n+125}{25}
=\frac{1}{25}\Bigl(24\sum_{n=101}^{125}n+125\cdot25\Bigr).
\]
But \(\sum_{n=101}^{125}n=25\cdot\frac{101+125}2=25\cdot113=2825\), so
\[
24\cdot2825+125\cdot25
=67800+3125=70925,
\]
and dividing by \(25\) gives
\[
\frac{70925}{25}=2837.
\]
Therefore the desired sum is \(\boxed{2837}\).
